mechanism_narrative: Declining federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window or positive return frequency drops below 55 percent during rate cuts.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 9 percent, labor market slack suppresses consumer spending and corporate earnings growth, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward returns exceed zero in more than 50 percent of instances where UNRATE surpasses the 9.0 percent threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in retail sales and stable consumer price indices indicate robust demand without inflationary pressure, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint signal yields negative forward returns in over 60 percent of triggered months or the interaction term loses statistical significance in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indices signal strengthening manufacturing output and supply-side momentum, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next week during expansionary phases.
failure_condition: Short-term returns turn negative in more than 55 percent of rising INDPRO observations within the specified regime.

mechanism_narrative: Increasing housing starts reflect growing consumer confidence and construction sector activity, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.01 over rolling 3-year windows or positive return frequency drops below 50 percent during housing upturns.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index exceeds 225, accelerating inflation erodes real corporate margins and prompts tighter monetary policy expectations, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in over 55 percent of months where CPIAUCSL breaches the 225 threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in total nonfarm payrolls and declining unemployment rates confirm genuine labor market tightening, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The combined signal produces negative forward returns in more than 60 percent of observations or fails to outperform single-proxy baselines in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: Quarter-over-quarter growth in real gross domestic product confirms macroeconomic expansion, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter during sustained growth regimes.
failure_condition: Forward returns are negative in more than 50 percent of quarters following positive GDPC1 revisions within the specified regime.
